How We Built a SaaS with $0/mo Infrastructure

Published March 21, 2026 · 8 min read

The Problem: SaaS Economics Are Broken

Traditional SaaS costs before you serve a single customer:

  • AWS/GCP/Azure: $100-500/mo just for minimum viable infrastructure
  • Database: $20-100/mo (even small Postgres instances)
  • Storage: $10-50/mo (S3, GCS)
  • Monitoring/Logging: $50-200/mo (Datadog, New Relic)
  • Total baseline: ~$500/mo before a single paying customer

This kills bootstrapping. Most founders abandon SaaS ideas because the fixed costs are too high.

Our Stack: Cloudflare's Free Tier Superpower

We built Mekong RaaS on Cloudflare's edge network. Here's why it's the game-changer:

1. Cloudflare Pages ($0)

Frontend hosting with unlimited requests, auto-HTTPS, instant deployment from git.

git push origin main
→ Vercel/Cloudflare triggers
→ Astro build (30s)
→ Pages deploys globally
→ Live instantly
→ 0 cost

2. Cloudflare Workers ($0/month standard)

Edge functions. Deploy API endpoints globally. 100K free requests/day. After that, $0.50 per 1M requests.

// wrangler.toml
[env.production]
routes = [
  { pattern = "api.example.com/*", zone_name = "example.com" }
]

// src/index.ts
export default {
  async fetch(request) {
    const auth = request.headers.get('Authorization')
    if (!auth) return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 })

    // Route to D1 database
    const db = env.DB
    const result = await db.prepare(
      'SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?'
    ).bind(userId).first()

    return Response.json(result)
  }
}

Our volume: 50K API calls/day in beta → $0 cost. We don't pay until 100K+.

3. Cloudflare D1 (SQLite on Edge) ($0)

Distributed SQLite with instant global replication. No VPC, no scaling limits on free tier.

// Create D1 database
wrangler d1 create production

// Schema: 3 tables, 2 indexes
CREATE TABLE users (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  email TEXT UNIQUE,
  mcu_balance INT DEFAULT 0,
  created_at DATETIME
);

CREATE TABLE missions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT,
  status TEXT,
  cost_mcu INT,
  FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
);

Why it matters: Zero cold-start latency. SQL is fast on edge. No database connection pooling needed.

4. Cloudflare KV (Global Cache) ($0)

Distributed key-value store for session tokens, rate limiting, user preferences. 100K free ops/day.

// Cache JWT tokens with 1hr expiry
const token = 'eyJhbGc...'
await env.KV.put('token:' + user_id, token, {
  expirationTtl: 3600
})

// Rate limiting: 100 requests per minute
const key = 'ratelimit:' + user_id + ':' + minute
const count = await env.KV.get(key) || 0
if (count > 100) return new Response('Too many', { status: 429 })
await env.KV.put(key, count + 1, { expirationTtl: 60 })

5. Cloudflare R2 (Object Storage) ($0.15/GB, no egress)

S3 alternative with zero egress costs. Store user mission results, logs, generated artifacts.

The Real Economics

Component Traditional Cloudflare
Compute (API) $200/mo $0 (under 100K/day)
Database $100/mo $0 (D1 free tier)
Cache/Sessions $50/mo $0 (KV free tier)
Storage $50/mo $5 (R2 at scale)
Monitoring $100/mo $0 (CF dashboard)
TOTAL (beta scale) $500/mo $5/mo

Our actual spend (March 2026): $2.47/mo. We're on the free tier for everything except a tiny R2 bill.

What We Learned

  • Edge-first changes everything: No VPC, no connection pooling, no cold starts. Just instant global requests.
  • SQLite scales: D1 replicates your DB to every CF datacenter. Reads are local. Writes are replicated.
  • KV is your secret weapon: Rate limiting, sessions, temporary data—all at edge speed with no backend hits.
  • Monitoring is free: CF dashboard gives you metrics, errors, and analytics. You don't need expensive APM tools at this scale.

Try Mekong RaaS Free

Deploy your own zero-cost SaaS. 10 free credits, no card required.
